mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate indicates tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer income and spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03, or the signal fails to predict positive forward returns in at least 55% of months with declining unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a threshold of 4.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when the trigger is active, or the average return during trigger periods is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in industrial production and retail sales signals robust aggregate demand that drives corporate profits and stock prices higher over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS post positive m/m growth for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When both proxies are rising, the median forward 20-day return is not greater than zero, or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55% over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Large positive quarterly GDP growth rates after a recession signal a self-sustaining expansion that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices over the medium term. This mechanism is most active during the early recovery phase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 0 for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: In quarters following the trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative more often than positive, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A falling industrial production index signals contraction in manufacturing output and economic weakness, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The direction of the relationship reverses: negative INDPRO growth is associated with positive forward 5-day returns for more than 40% of episodes over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, the labor market becomes very tight, leading to wage inflation and compressed profit margins, which pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4% and the unemployment rate is below 5%, the economy is overheating and the central bank is likely to tighten further, creating headwinds for equities and negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: In months when both conditions hold, the median 20-day forward return is greater than zero, or the proportion of negative returns is not above 55% over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year growth below 2%), a moderate rise in consumer prices signals reflation and improving nominal demand, which lifts corporate revenues and equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, a positive change in CPI is not followed by positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or the correlation between CPI change and forward returns is non-positive.
